title: Extra Credit Assignment
description:
Assignment: To start, pick EITHER a war or a treaty from the war list Download war list or the treaty list Download treaty list; only the listed wars and treaties qualify for getting credit on this assignment. You can only receive credit for writing up a report on ONE treaty OR ONE war, not for both (i.e., no doubling up).

War Report 

Part #1, for 5 midterm exam points equivalent:

In your own words, write up a report on the war you have chosen, identifying the war by its name and Correlates of War (COW) number, the countries involved, their respective leaders, years in which the war was fought, the approximate number of casualties, and the key issues over which the war was fought.

Part #2, for an additional 5 points (i.e. 10 midterm exam points equivalent total if you do both parts of this assignment): 

Explain how the war started, the outcome of the war (i.e. who won/lost, what did they win/lose, was there a settlement, etc.), and whether allies got involved and on whose side.

Use reputable sources for this information and include a full/proper bibliography and in-text citations as needed. 

Format: 1-2 single-spaced, typewritten pages. 

Worth: up to 10 midterm exam points equivalent added as a separate extra credit grade category (i.e. 2.5% of the course grade max).

Due: Turn in via Canvas by Monday, December 1st. Late assignments accepted through Friday, December 5th for 1/2 credit; 0 thereafter.

 

Treaty Report

Part #1, for 5 midterm exam points equivalent:

In your own words, write up a report on the treaty you have chosen, identifying the treaty by its name, and provide background information (actors, date of passage, IO(s) involved, goals of the treaty) on it from a reputable source

Part #2, for an additional 5 points (i.e. 10 midterm exam points equivalent total if you do both parts of this assignment): 

Explain the background of what led to the treaty being signed and the effectiveness of the agreement since (e.g., has the treaty aided in advancing the goal it was created for, have other countries joined, has anyone removed themselves from the treaty, etc.) 

Use reputable sources for this information and include a full/proper bibliography and in-text citations as needed. 

Format: 1-2 single-spaced, typewritten pages. 

Worth: up to 10 midterm exam points equivalent added as a separate extra credit grade category (i.e. 2.5% of the course grade max).

Due: Turn in via Canvas by 5 pm Monday, December 1st. Late assignments accepted through Friday, December 5th for 1/2 credit; 0 thereafter.
